user_query:
Please generate Java source code for two database tables using EasyCode-style templates. Read the generation spec from input/easycode_spec.json. Use the Custom-V2 template group, the provided author and base_package, and the provided table and column metadata (don’t try to connect to a database). For each table, convert the snake_case table name to PascalCase for class names (e.g., user_account -> UserAccount). Generate the following files for each table under output/src/main/java mapped from the base package (com.acme.shop -> output/src/main/java/com/acme/shop):
- domain/repository/po/{TableName}PO.java
- domain/repository/mapper/{TableName}Mapper.java
- domain/entity/{TableName}.java
- domain/repository/service/{TableName}Repository.java
- domain/factory/{TableName}Factory.java
- domain/service/{TableName}Service.java
- application/service/{TableName}ApplicationService.java
- interfaces/dto/{TableName}DTO.java
- interfaces/dto/{TableName}EditDTO.java
- interfaces/dto/{TableName}PageDTO.java
- interfaces/viewobject/{TableName}VO.java
Follow Custom-V2’s package conventions (e.g., domain.repository.po, domain.repository.mapper, domain.entity, etc.). In PO classes, include @TableName("<table_name>") and declare the primary key field. In Mapper interfaces, extend BaseMapper<{TableName}PO>. Use sensible default type mappings (Number/INTEGER->Long/Integer; DECIMAL->BigDecimal; TIMESTAMP/DATE->Date; VARCHAR->String) based on the column types in the spec. Also produce:
- output/plan.json: a machine-readable plan that includes base_package, template_group, a per-table section with class names and the full list of generated file paths.
- output/GENERATION_REPORT.md: a human-readable summary covering: the resolved base package, template group, table-to-class naming decisions, type mapping assumptions, and a clear statement that no plaintext passwords were stored. If any files would be overwritten, list them under an "Overwrite Candidates" section.
Only write files under output/. Do not store or echo any credentials.
